Yeah, I'm making this decision for them because people are afraid of decisions. So I'm like, don't make that decision today. Make it in months from now. I'm pushing out their decision later. You don't have to, your only decision is like, do I think this might work? If it might work, then just try it. Pushing that decision out too late. They're like, cool, I'll decide about later. I'm just gonna try it then, right? Next thing is, I want them to understand this is not about me, but it's about them. Okay, one of my favorite copywriters ever, his name is Johan Mock, lives in Singapore. First copywriter I paid, I paid $8,000 for sales letter, which was like, all the money I made in the world back then. And then we never launched the product. That was a bad idea, it was horrible. But I became obsessed with his writing. So I used to read all of his sales letters. He used to always use this in every clothes he did for every single sales letter. He would come back and basically position it, where it's just like, look, whether you buy this product or not, it does not change my life whatsoever. Okay, this is not about me, this is about you. So me guys are wondering, oh, Russell's gonna make a thousand bucks from all these people and he's gonna be all rich. It doesn't matter, who cares? This is not about me. My life will not change, when you're $9,000 and $7,000, I'm fine. But this is about you, that $9,000 and $7,000 will change your life. I want them to understand that. Because it takes a lot, some people always have this weird thing like, why don't I wanna give him money because whatever, take that away and help them realize this is a decision about them, not about giving Russell money. I'm taking that away from them. And then yeah, putting out the risk group or stuff out there. Any questions about that at all? Most people come to the Guarantee slide and they're like, I have a 30-year guarantee and they move on. I spent more time on this than I did explaining my course. Right? We've been putting in, I'm just scared to do the 30-day back guarantee, we didn't do it and then we threw it in and I was saying, only if you do your homework and submit that you actually watched it. I don't know what you think about that and I know some people do it, some people don't. I think it's good, especially if refunds are high, there's things you can do. But in the end of the day, like the world we live in, credit card companies don't care about you as a merchant, they care about the consumers. If they want their money back, they get back anyway. If you look at how do businesses like ours get in trouble, the only way you're going to get in trouble is you don't give back people's money. If you're going to give people's money, then they complain to BBB. The BBB then goes to the FTC, excuse me, BBB goes to turn general, turn general goes to FTC and they put you in jail. If you get everyone the money back, you never go to jail. So for me, I'm like, I don't want to go to jail, so I just give everyone the money back. So if I'm going to do it anyway, might as well sell it as a benefit, that's my thoughts. I had maybe three, when they fight you through the straight thing, and no, I'm just giving them the money back sometimes, I fight it back, but as far as giving them the money back, they're... Like typing the numbers like, waiting, and I'm like, ah, I gotta hurry and do it, because they just go crazy the whole entire time, and about 30 minutes is the part where like, for me, like about 30 minutes is when the cells kind of start dropping off to where it's not really that big of an impact afterwards, okay? So I don't have a payment plan, but this is a really cool thing. One time I needed to invest in a course like this, right, and have the money, and I had a friend, I actually emailed the company, asked if they would finance it for me, and they roll back and said, hey, we don't actually finance it, but there's two companies we'll finance it for, one's called Visa and one's called MasterCard. And I was like, well, what do you mean? I was like, hey, that's actually a good thing, but when I was wrestling at, I was a wrestler growing up, I wrestled at BYU, and the head coach of BYU is Mark Schultz, how many of you guys have ever seen Fox Catcher, the movie? Okay, so my coach was played by, what's his name? The Fancy Pants guy in that movie. Yeah, yeah, Chadam Taney actually played my coach in that movie, and my coach's brother was murdered in that movie, so it's a true story, and that guy was actually my coach at BYU, and if you know Mark Schultz in his story, he was an Olympic champ, one of the toughest human beings on planet Earth, he was helping some guy train for the UFC, and the night before the match, the guy who was training with broke his hand. My coach had never been in the UFC, never fought professionally his entire life, but his partner, he was drilling with, broke his hand, and they called him, and I said, hey, Mark, who's really gonna fight in the UFC tomorrow? And my coach, who's the toughest man on planet Earth, was like, yeah, I don't mind, I'll do that, so you stepped on the mat, never doing any kind of UFC in the world fights this big, huge, tall, like six foot six black man. My coach picks him up, slams him on the ground, beats the crap out of him live TV in his very first fight in UFC nine, like you watch the video, it's insane, I show pictures of it sometimes, and I'm like, so that was my coach, and my first week at BYU, he came to me, he was coaching me, and then that night, I think it was like three or four days into it, that night I got a knock on the door, right? In my dorms, so I opened the door, and it's my coach, and if you see him there, he's like five foot six, strongest man on Earth, pretty much, and he comes to the door super intimidating, and he walks in, and he's like, hey Russell, how's it going, am I good? And he hands me this video tape, and the title of the video tape was Total Violence by Mark Schultz, and he hands me this tape, I'm like, what's this, he's like, watch this video, I'm like, okay, and he's like, go get your wallet. I'm like, why do you want me to get my wallet? He said, just go get your wallet, he's like, okay, so I go and I get my wallet, I come back, and I hand it to my coach, and he opens it up, pulls out the dollars, and there's probably 20 bucks or so in loose dollars, pulls it out, puts it in his pocket, and hands back my wallet. I was like, coach, what are you doing? But don't forget, I'm scared to death this guy, literally killed me with his bare hands, I'm like, what are you doing? He's like, I'm gonna teach you a lesson right now. He said, if I gave you that video tape, you would take it and you put it on the shelf and you'd never watch it. He's like, but you just invested every single dollar you have in this tape, and now you're gonna watch it, and you're gonna learn from it, and you're becoming a better wrestler because of it. He was right, because I paid for it, because I invested, I watched it, and I became a better wrestler, and my coach changed my life because of that. So I don't want to deprive you of that ability, so I'm not gonna just give it to you because you're not gonna value it, okay, I'm gonna charge you just like I charge everybody else because all my success stories, they paid for it. If you pay for it, you'll value it, and you have success as well. So right now you gotta go to clickfunds.com slash on it. So I'm just breathing in these stories that resolve the false concerns for my life, from other people's life. I've had some of our students who have used my Coach Schultz story to resolve that concern for them. My coach, his name's Russell, and he's got this guy named Coach Schultz who beats people up and they tell that whole, they tell Russell's story, and it's like, oh, you can use other people's stories, it doesn't have to always be your own. So that's what I'm doing here for the next 30 minutes is I'm telling stories, answering concerns, called action over and over and over and over and over again. That's how I do that. Any questions on that at all? Can we do that over and over and over again? And that is kind of the process. So you just go through until it hits zero? Yeah. And so it's always remember that like whenever I'm on live, like Facebook live or TV or webinars or anything I'm doing, like I gotta be at this level for it to sound normal. What's your answer to the question? Who owns the data? Who owns the data? So there's so many filling, so I say, there's different filling networks. So I'm a feel it like, there's actually filling networks that when you sell, when you sell something to, like you sell a customer, they actually retain the rights to that customer and they will mail them and sell them products and services. For us, we don't own the data. It'd be unethical and illegal. So if you send customers in, they're your customers, I don't message them on email, I don't follow them, I don't have anything like those customers are your customers, your data's safe and secure and it's yours. When we first started, that was a big concern people had is that we were gonna like steal their customers or like we were gonna, we were doing this whole thing so we could get the leads for ourselves. Yeah, the more, we had a guy, I can't remember his name now, he was doing a running webinar for like 300 bucks. And he would, he gets so mad afterwards people didn't buy. And they would do, they would buy. So the problem with us as marketers is we know all this stuff we're doing, and so we're assuming that everybody can see what we're doing. They can't. It's a vacuum. Somebody's on Facebook. They see an ad. They click on it. They register for a webinar. They go through this process, and they go through the whole thing, and then it's gone. And yeah, if they go back and find the ad and click on the link, they can come back through and they can start the process again. But they're in this vacuum where this is happening. And what I do is basically Sunday at midnight, the page where the webinar replays at disappears. And for me, like in my email sequence, the last day here in the email, I'm like, OK, there's two things you need. Number one, go watch the webinar now. But number two, if you're ready, sign up. So I give them both the order form link and the webinar link. And so the webinar link disappears. I never shut down my order form link. So if someone comes back later and they click on it, they can still give me money. But this page disappears. I say the webinar is expiring. This webinar is gone. I don't kill the offer, but I kill the webinar is gone. That way, you still get cells trickling afterwards. But for them, in this vacuum, it's gone. Like that. What page is it going?
